novel_features.name: Counter-pool analysis
novel_features.description: Given our champion pool and the enemy pool, return the full matchup matrix ranked by WR delta with sample-size floor — the SQL coaches do in their head.
novel_features.rationale: Mobalytics, u.gg, and op.gg all show counters one champion at a time. The cross-product of two pools is a single SQL join over the local champion_matchups table; no aggregator page exposes it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent or coach needs to know 'given two confirmed champion pools, who picks into whom.' Avoids N×M page loads.

novel_features.name: Patch meta-shift
novel_features.description: Diff two tier_snapshots and list champions that gained or lost ≥1 tier or ≥2% WR since a prior patch, with sample-size guard.
novel_features.rationale: Every aggregator shows the current patch's tier list. None of them show what moved. The diff is trivial against a local store that synced two snapshots.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent or coach needs to answer 'what changed this patch' without manually diffing tier-list screenshots.

novel_features.name: Head-to-head champion compare
novel_features.description: Side-by-side join across tier, build, counters, and matchups for two champions — including item-overlap percentage.
novel_features.rationale: No aggregator renders two champions on one page. The query joins three local tables for the chosen patch/role/rank.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Resolves draft-time 'which of these two is better right now' questions in one command.

novel_features.name: ARAM batch item-set export
novel_features.description: Write LoL client item-set JSON for N champions in ARAM mode in one command, refreshing all stale files in the client config folder.
novel_features.rationale: Championify did this for Summoner's Rift in 2019 and died; no maintained successor exists for ARAM. The build data is already in the local store after sync.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an ARAM player wants game-ready item-sets across their reroll pool without alt-tabbing between champ select and a website.

novel_features.name: Duo-finder with candidate-pool filter
novel_features.description: Best support pairings for a given ADC, restricted to a candidate support pool — coaches' player pools are fixed.
novel_features.rationale: u.gg has a duo-finder but it ranks across all supports; coaches need 'best from these three I can actually run.'
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the support pool is fixed (coach, duo queue, ranked flex) and you need WR-ranked picks from that subset.

novel_features.name: Personal pool tier digest
novel_features.description: For each champion in your pool: current tier, WR delta since last patch, top-1 counter, top-1 synergy — all in one composite query.
novel_features.rationale: Daily-check-in commands collapse multi-tab morning rituals into one read. Composite query over four local tables, no equivalent on any aggregator.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as a morning ritual or pre-queue command to surface what changed about your specific champions.

novel_features.name: Power-spike phase filter
novel_features.description: List champions ranked by early/mid/late spike strength for a chosen role — inverts Mobalytics's per-champion data into a leaderboard.
novel_features.rationale: Mobalytics shows power-spike phase on each champion page but never aggregates 'who spikes early in jungle.' Inverting it requires the data in a local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when picking around a teammate's pick (ARAM, draft) to find spike-aligned champions.

novel_features.name: Flex-pick detector
novel_features.description: Champions that are ≥A-tier in 2+ roles for the same patch and rank — the SQL no aggregator surfaces because they index by role first.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregators all start with role → champion list. The inverse (champion → list of roles where they are strong) requires a self-join over tier_snapshots.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when drafting champions who don't tip your hand about role — invaluable for fearless-draft formats and amateur scrims.

novel_features.name: Region-split tier compare
novel_features.description: Same patch, same rank — three regions side-by-side. Surfaces pick-priority drift between KR/EUW/NA that no aggregator defaults to.
novel_features.rationale: Every aggregator scopes a tier-list view to one region per page-load. The multi-region pivot is one SQL with the regions as columns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to spot regional meta divergence — KR is usually 2 weeks ahead of NA, and this surfaces it in one view.

narrative.headline: Every League of Legends aggregator, plus a local SQLite for cross-champion queries no single page surfaces.
narrative.value_prop: mobalytics-lol-pp-cli pulls champion data, builds, runes, counters, and tier ratings from Mobalytics and Riot Data Dragon, hydrates them into a local SQLite store, then exposes cross-champion SQL queries no aggregator page surfaces: counter-pool matrices, patch meta-shift deltas, head-to-head compares, region-split tier views, and ARAM batch item-set exports for the LoL client. Free, offline-after-sync, agent-native (--json + --select + --agent), and the only LoL data source agentically accessible without a headless browser.

quickstart.comment: Hydrate the local store with the latest patch's champions, items, runes, tier snapshots, builds, and matchups
Look up the recommended Aatrox top build for Emerald+ — the single most common ritual
Diamond+ mid-lane tier list with WR/PR/BR
Pool-vs-pool matchup matrix coaches do in their head
What moved up or down since two patches ago

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results from any Mobalytics command
troubleshoots.fix: Run `mobalytics-lol-pp-cli sync --full` once after first install; the local store has to be hydrated before queries return data.
Run `mobalytics-lol-pp-cli sync --resources tier_snapshots --since 1d` to refresh; tier data moves daily as games are played.
Mobalytics GraphQL rejects requests without `Origin: https://mobalytics.gg`; verify with `mobalytics-lol-pp-cli doctor`.

troubleshoots.symptom: item-set --to client doesn't show in League client
troubleshoots.fix: Restart the League client after writing item sets; the client only reads the config folder on startup. Use `--to file` for ad-hoc JSON export instead.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs aggregator-quality LoL champion data without a headless browser, when you want to compose cross-champion queries (pool vs pool, patch diffs, flex pickers) no aggregator page surfaces, or when you want ARAM/SR item-sets exported to the LoL client in batch. Reach for it during draft analysis, agentic Discord-bot prompts about 'who beats X this patch', morning-ritual pool digests, or coach pre-scrim preparation. Don't use it for live game data (Riot's match API is the right source) or personal summoner profiles (Mobalytics Plus's overlay is the right tool).

recipes.title: Diamond+ jungle picks that spike early
recipes.explanation: Inverts Mobalytics's per-champion power-spike data into a leaderboard scoped by role and rank — useful when picking around a teammate's pick or when an agent needs to recommend early-game junglers for a side comp.

recipes.title: Pre-scrim pool-vs-pool matrix as JSON
recipes.explanation: Coaches' classic question — given our 3 picks and their 3 picks in top lane, what's the full 3×3 matchup matrix with sample sizes. The --select keeps only the four columns an agent needs.

recipes.title: Export ARAM item-sets to the LoL client for tonight's session
recipes.explanation: Replaces the dead Championify workflow for ARAM: one command writes 7 game-ready item-set JSON files into the LoL client config folder. No browser, no copy-paste, restart client to use.

recipes.title: What moved up since last patch in mid lane
recipes.explanation: Diffs the current tier_snapshots against the snapshot from patch 16.9. The --agent flag returns structured JSON of winners (gained ≥1 tier) and losers, with sample guard so noise gets filtered.

recipes.title: Agent draft prompt — head-to-head with field narrowing
recipes.explanation: A Discord-bot or Claude-driven draft assistant pipes this through jq to surface 'which is the better top pick right now' in one call. The --select keeps the payload tight enough for agent context windows even when both champions have 50+ matchup rows in the underlying tables.

trigger_phrases: what changed in the meta this patch
